other_observers: Rachel Hopper, Norm Erthal, Joey Kellner, Larry Semo, Nathan Pieplow, Andrew Spencer, Glenn Walbek, et al.; Cole Wild originally found this bird on the Ft. Collins CBC, 12/17/2005, with two other observers.
specific_location: East side of the wastewater treatment plant on Hwy 14, just west of LeMay Avenue, along the Cache la Pouder River.
description: Small passerine foraging along a stream. Solid olive-brown upperparts; head had a thin, even width, buffy supercilium, olive-brown eyestripe, and splotchy cheek patch. Underparts were basally buffy-white, with bold distinct olive-brown streaks extending from chin to belly and along the flanks. Bill was short, pointed, and dark. Legs were dark with a bit of a fleshy-pink tone.Behaviors: Observed foraging along a stream coming out of a wastewater treatment plant. Bird occasionally would teeter its tail, not its entire body like a Spotted Sandpiper. Mostly stayed along the water's edge, but did fly up onto the bank to forage around exposed tree roots on two occasions.Call: none heardPlumage: unknown
